papers.title: Dilution reference: solute amount vs concentration and volume (chemistry slice)
The Adsorption of Gases on Plane Surfaces of Glass, Mica and Platinum
Sum of two even natural numbers is even (reference slice)
Stress test: approximations and asymptotic regimes (intake scaffold)
Stress test: dimensional analysis and unit systems (intake scaffold)
Kinetics of ammonia synthesis on promoted iron catalysts (Temkin-Pyzhev slice)

claims.informal_text: When a solution is diluted by increasing volume by a positive factor k while solute amount is fixed, concentration scales inversely: c₀ v₀ = (c₀/k)(v₀ k) for positive rational concentration, volume, a
If two solution states carry the same fixed solute amount so that c₀ v₀ = c₁ v₁ with v₁ ≠ 0, then c₁ = (c₀ v₀)/v₁.
Empirical Freundlich relation: adsorbed amount per unit mass scales as concentration to the power 1/n times a constant k.
For nonnegative k and positive concentration and exponent denominator n, the Freundlich isotherm value is nonnegative.
For strictly positive k, c, and n, the Freundlich isotherm is strictly positive.
When k is zero, the Freundlich isotherm is zero for any positive c and n.
When concentration is zero and n is positive, the Freundlich isotherm is zero.
For fixed positive k and n, the isotherm is monotone nondecreasing in concentration.
For fixed positive c and n, the isotherm is monotone nondecreasing in k.
At unit concentration, the isotherm equals k when n is positive.
Scaling k by a nonnegative factor scales the isotherm by the same factor.
The concentration power term c^(1/n) is positive when c and n are positive.
For positive k and n, if c1 < c2 then the isotherm at c1 is strictly less than at c2.
Doubling k doubles the isotherm for fixed positive c and n.
At equilibrium, the surface coverage is determined by a ratio-form expression in pressure and an adsorption parameter.
Langmuir coverage is nonnegative for nonnegative K and P.
Langmuir coverage is at most 1 when K and P are nonnegative.
At zero pressure, coverage is zero.
When the adsorption constant is zero, coverage is zero.
Denominator 1 + K P is positive or K P is zero.
Coverage is positive for positive K and P.
Coverage is monotone increasing in pressure.
Coverage is monotone increasing in adsorption constant.
Coverage plus reciprocal of denominator equals one.
One minus coverage equals reciprocal of denominator.
At P = 1/K coverage is one half.
Coverage times denominator equals K P.
Scaling K by c is equivalent to scaling P by c.
Relation between coverage at -P and at -K.
One minus coverage is reciprocal of denominator.
Numerator K P is nonnegative for nonnegative K and P.
Reciprocal of denominator equals one minus coverage.
At P = 1/K coverage equals one half.
Coverage at (0,0) is zero.
The sum of two even natural numbers is even.
Under constant acceleration, final velocity u + a t equals u + t a over rationals.
Displacement u t + (1/2) a t^2 rearranges to t (u + (1/2) a t).
Velocity change over interval dt: (u + a dt) - u = a dt.
Displacement over 2T equals twice displacement over T plus a T^2 (kinematics identity in ℚ).
Sum of two equal halves of (u+v) equals u+v.
Acceleration from velocity change: (dv/dt)*dt = dv when dt is nonzero.
Squared final velocity (u + a t)^2 expands to u^2 + 2 u a t + (a t)^2 in ℚ.
Torricelli-style identity: (u + a t)^2 = u^2 + 2 a (u t + ½ a t^2) for uniform acceleration in ℚ.
With zero acceleration, u + 0·t = u (velocity unchanged in ℚ).
At t = 0, displacement u·0 + ½ a·0^2 = 0.
Average of initial and final speeds times t equals u t + ½ a t^2 when v = u + a t.
Velocity composition: u + a(t + s) = (u + a t) + a s for fixed a in ℚ.
Standard displacement formula is additive in initial velocity: (u1+u2)t + ½ a t^2 = u1 t + u2 t + ½ a t^2.
Doubling the half-quadratic term: 2·(½ a t^2) = a t^2 in ℚ.
Mean acceleration ((u + a Δt) - u) / Δt equals a when Δt ≠ 0 in ℚ.
Temkin coverage relation is modeled as theta = (R*T/a) * ln(K*p).
Coverage is zero at reciprocal pressure when K is nonzero.
Equivalent unit-product condition K*p = 1 implies zero logarithmic coverage term.
With positive coefficient and positive K, the Temkin response is strictly increasing in pressure.
The same positivity regime yields nondecreasing pressure response.
At fixed pressure and positive coefficient, response is strictly increasing in equilibrium constant K.
Scaling gas constant scales Temkin response linearly.
Scaling absolute temperature scales Temkin response linearly.
Changing sign of interaction parameter flips the sign of the coverage value.
For positive factors, ln(K*p) decomposes into ln(K)+ln(p).
Temkin relation can be rewritten as affine form in log-pressure with constant log-K offset.
If coefficient is nonnegative and K*p >= 1, then logarithmic coverage value is nonnegative.
